product_overview: TripoMist is a React-based travel booking web app that helps users discover destinations, browse group and weekend trips, review detailed itineraries, and complete bookings online. The experience is centered on quick exploration, trip comparison, itinerary download, and checkout with prefilled trip details.
core_goals: Enable travelers to explore destinations and trip offerings from a polished home experience
Allow users to browse, search, and filter group trips and view upcoming departures
Provide detailed itinerary pages with downloadable trip plans
Support a streamlined checkout flow using trip name and price from URL query parameters
Offer intuitive navigation across the app through a responsive navbar and quick action links

features.name: Home Page Experience
features.description: The home page introduces TripoMist with a branded hero section, destination discovery, featured trips, trip highlights, and quick contact actions.
user_flows: GET / to load the home page -> Receive hero section, destination circles, featured trips, stats banner, and floating action buttons -> User clicks Explore Group Trips CTA -> Navigate to /group-trips
GET / to load the home page -> Receive destination cards with pricing -> User clicks a featured trip Book Now button -> Navigate to /checkout?trip={tripName}&price={amount}
GET / to load the home page -> Receive floating call and WhatsApp buttons -> User taps a contact button -> Open phone call or WhatsApp contact action
GET / to load the home page -> Receive home content successfully
GET / to load the home page -> Home page fails to render required assets/content -> Show an error state or fallback UI

features.name: Navigation Bar
features.description: The navbar provides persistent navigation to major site sections and supports mobile-friendly menu access.
user_flows: GET / to load the app shell with navbar -> Receive Home, Group Trips, Weekend Trips, and Upcoming Trips links -> User clicks Group Trips -> Navigate to /group-trips
GET /group-trips to view a section of the app -> User opens mobile hamburger menu -> Receive expanded navigation links -> User clicks Upcoming Trips -> Navigate to /upcoming-trips
GET / to load the navbar -> Receive brand logo linking home -> User clicks logo -> Navigate to /
GET / to load the navbar -> Navbar links fail to display or route correctly -> Show navigation error/fallback state

features.name: Group Trips Browsing and Search
features.description: The group trips page lets users browse available trips and narrow options by destination or trip style.
user_flows: GET /group-trips to load available trips -> Receive trip listings with search and filter controls -> User enters a destination search term or selects a style filter -> Trip list updates to matching results
GET /group-trips to browse trips -> User selects a trip from the results -> Navigate to /itinerary/{id} for itinerary details
GET /group-trips to load available trips -> Receive no matching trips for the selected filter/search -> Show empty-state message with no results
GET /group-trips to load the page -> Group trips data fails to load or filter/search interaction fails -> Show an error state or retry option

features.name: Weekend Trips Listings
features.description: The weekend trips page displays available short-duration trip packages for users looking for quick getaways.
user_flows: GET /weekend-trips to load weekend package listings -> Receive weekend trip cards/packages -> User reviews available options on the page
GET /weekend-trips to load weekend packages -> Weekend trip data fails to load -> Show an error state or fallback message

features.name: Upcoming Trips Departures
features.description: The upcoming trips page shows scheduled trip departures with dates so users can plan ahead.
user_flows: GET /upcoming-trips to load departures -> Receive a list of upcoming trips with dates -> User browses departure options
GET /upcoming-trips to load departures -> No upcoming departures are available -> Show an empty-state message
GET /upcoming-trips to load departures -> Upcoming departures data fails to load -> Show an error state or retry option

features.name: Itinerary Detail and Download
features.description: The itinerary page provides a day-by-day trip plan and allows users to download the itinerary through a modal.
user_flows: GET /itinerary/{id} to load a trip itinerary -> Receive detailed day-by-day plan and download itinerary action -> User opens download modal -> POST /itinerary/{id}/download (or equivalent download action) -> Receive itinerary file or download confirmation
GET /itinerary/{id} to load a trip itinerary -> User opens download modal -> Download request fails or itinerary id is invalid -> Show error message in modal or itinerary page
GET /itinerary/{id} with an unknown id -> Itinerary is not found -> Show not-found or error state

features.name: Checkout and Booking Completion
features.description: The checkout page collects traveler details, summarizes the trip, and completes the booking using trip and price query parameters.
user_flows: GET /checkout?trip={tripName}&price={amount} to open checkout -> Receive prefilled booking summary with trip name and price -> User fills traveller information form -> Submit booking -> Receive booking confirmation or payment completion
GET /checkout?trip={tripName}&price={amount} to open checkout -> User submits the form with missing required traveller information -> Receive validation errors and remain on checkout
GET /checkout without valid trip or price query params -> Checkout cannot build a valid summary -> Show error state or request user to return from a trip page
GET /checkout?trip={tripName}&price={amount} to open checkout -> Payment or booking submission fails -> Show booking failure message and allow retry

frontend: React Router v6 (BrowserRouter, Routes, Route, Link, useNavigate)

pages.name: Home
features: Hero section with CTA buttons (Explore Group Trips, See Upcoming Departures)
Destination circles (Spain, Japan, Vietnam, Bali, Thailand, Ladakh, Spiti Valley, Andaman)
Camera roll destination cards (Ladakh, Spiti Valley, Kashmir) with pricing
Featured Group Trips section (Ladakh Expedition, Spiti Circuit, Kashmir Retreat) with Book Now buttons
Stats banner (4.9 Google Rating, 10K+ travellers, 100+ trips)

pages.name: Checkout
features: Trip details from URL query params (trip name, price)

components.name: Navbar
features: Navigation links (Home, Group Trips, Weekend Trips, Upcoming Trips)

key_user_flows: Browse home page -> Click destination -> View group trips or itinerary
Browse featured trips -> Click "Book Now" -> Checkout with pre-filled trip info
Navigate via Navbar to any section
